calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-12-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Oct-27-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
ROBBIE G. MUNOZ, Esq., STALWART LAW GROUP, APC (310) 954-2000 robbie@stalwartlaw.com Attorneys for Plaintiff, JULIO CASTILLO

Katrina C. Decker, Esq., VOGL MEREDITH BURKE & STREZA LLP (415) 398-0200 kdecker@vmbllp.com
Attorney for Defendant SAFEWAY INC., erroneously sued as ALBERTSONS SAFEWAY, LLC

Hearing held. Discussed, not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JULIO CASTILLO MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS

Plaintiff Julio Castillo's motion to quash deposition subpoenas is granted and his request for monetary sanctions is granted in small part. Although Safeway may have good cause to have non-expert discovery reopened to allow for the service of the subpoenas and production of records responsive to the subpoenas, non-expert discovery closed prior to the issuance of each of the four subpoenas. Safeway's request in its opposition papers to file a motion to reopen fact discovery on shortened time must be made by an ex parte application and is not cognizable based on opposition papers alone. Because Safeway acted without substantial justification in issuing the subpoenas, Castillo is entitled to an award of sanctions. Safeway and its counsel must pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $600 to Castillo no later than April 30, 2026.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling order in compliance with CRC3.1312.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)
